title: Ochna antunesii
taxonomy: Ochna antunesii belongs to the family Ochnaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the chafftree or hook flowers. The genus Ochna is a part of this family, comprising about 70 species of plants. This genus is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, particularly in Africa and Asia. Ochna antunesii is a species within this genus, and its exact classification is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, order Malpighiales, family Ochnaceae, genus Ochna, species Ochna antunesii. Further classification details may be available through specific scientific studies or databases.

intro: Ochna antunesii, also known as the Ochna tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, native to tropical Africa. The Ochna tree has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate various health conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ochna antunesii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ochna antunesii is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
